responses.content_en: Based on the symptoms described, this appears to be a case of contact dermatitis. I recommend applying a topical corticosteroid cream and avoiding the allergen. If symptoms persist, oral antihistamines may help reduce itching and inflammation.
The rash and itching pattern suggest contact dermatitis. Treatment includes topical steroids and identification of the triggering allergen. Consider patch testing if the cause is unclear.

The persistent cough and fever may indicate pneumonia. A chest X-ray is recommended to confirm the diagnosis. Empirical antibiotic therapy with amoxicillin should be started while awaiting culture results.
Given the symptoms of cough and high fever lasting more than a week, bacterial pneumonia should be considered. Recommend chest radiography and sputum culture. Start antibiotics promptly.
The blood glucose levels indicate type 2 diabetes. Lifestyle modifications including diet and exercise are the first line of treatment. Metformin is recommended as initial pharmacotherapy.
Elevated fasting glucose and HbA1c confirm diabetes mellitus type 2. Begin with dietary counseling, regular physical activity, and metformin therapy. Monitor blood glucose regularly.
